acceptInvite.description: Accept the invite to become a member of this workspace.

accountPage.title: Account
accountPage.signedOutHint: We couldn't verify your sign-in. Please sign in again.

activityFeed.emptyHint: Runs and scorecards show up here once you run them.

analyzeScorecards.customSummary: {total, plural, one {# scorecard} other {# scorecards}} · live from current data
analyzeScorecards.emptyHintNoHarness: No completed scorecards for this harness. Try another harness.
analyzeScorecards.harnessesDesc: Compare harness performance across each benchmark at a glance.
analyzeScorecards.modelsDesc: Compare per-benchmark performance across models on the same harness.
analyzeScorecards.summary: {total, plural, one {# completed scorecard} other {# completed scorecards}} · pass rate · live from current data
analyzeScorecards.trendDesc: Pick a harness to see per-benchmark score changes over the last 20 days.

createSchedule.cronHint: e.g. daily 03:00 = 0 3 * * *, every 15 min on weekdays = */15 * * * 1-5
createSchedule.timezoneHint: IANA tz (e.g. Asia/Seoul). Defaults to UTC.
createSchedule.overlapSkip: skip (skip if a run is in progress)
createSchedule.runtimeHint: Self-hosted (self:) runtimes need the runner online at fire time.
createSchedule.footerNote: Runs a scorecard on this dataset and harness on your schedule. It runs as you, and results are recorded in the workspace.

createWorkspace.nameHint: Use your team or org name. Its eval data is kept separately here.
createWorkspace.idHint: Auto-generated from the name if left blank:

datasetList.emptyHint: Try a different search or filter.
datasetList.creator: Created by

datasetsPage.title: Benchmarks
datasetsPage.description: Compare any harness fairly on the same problems.
datasetsPage.emptyHint: Create your first benchmark. Upload it directly or import from a source.
datasetsPage.registerDescription: Register evaluation cases in your workspace.
datasetsPage.noPermTitle: You don't have permission to register.
datasetsPage.noPermHint: Ask a workspace admin for access.
datasetsPage.importDescription: Build a benchmark straight from HuggingFace or JSONL. You can also import from the public catalog.
datasetsPage.importNoPermTitle: You don't have permission to add.
datasetsPage.defaultDescription: Evaluate any harness on the same problems.
datasetsPage.versionImmutableTip: Versions are immutable. To edit, create a new version.
datasetsPage.newVersionDescWithVersion: Loaded {id} v{version}. Change it and publish a new version.
datasetsPage.newVersionDescNoVersion: Loaded {id}. Change it and publish a new version.
datasetsPage.compareDescription: See how the cases and metadata differ between two versions.
datasetsPage.needTwoVersionsTitle: You need at least 2 versions to compare.
datasetsPage.needTwoVersionsHint: Create a new version to compare versions.

deleteWorkspace.title: Delete workspace
deleteWorkspace.cardDescription: The workspace and all its data will be gone. This can't be undone.
deleteWorkspace.dialogWarning: {name} and all its runs, datasets, harnesses, members, and secrets are permanently deleted. This can't be undone.

discuss.composerPlaceholder: Leave a note on this item… mention a member with @ to notify them
discuss.memberOnly: You need member access to comment.
discuss.title: Comments

downloadDesktop.loadFailedHint: You can't download here right now. Please grab it from the release page.
downloadDesktop.preparingHint: Once an admin sets it up, you can download right here.
downloadDesktop.inDesktopNote: You're already in the desktop app. Connect this machine from Account > Connected runners.
downloadDesktop.macNote: Get arm64 for Apple Silicon (M1+), x64 for Intel Macs.
downloadDesktop.step1: Launch the app and sign in with the same account as the web.
downloadDesktop.step2: In Account > Connected runners, just click Connect this machine as a runner.
downloadDesktop.step3: When running a scorecard, you can pick your computer as the runtime.
downloadDesktop.unsignedWarning: Installers aren't signed yet. On macOS right-click → Open, on Windows use SmartScreen ‘More info → Run’, and for the Linux AppImage run chmod +x first.

downloadPage.title: Desktop app
downloadPage.description: Everything the web does, plus this computer as a runner. Install it and connect once from your account.
downloadPage.errorInvalidAsset: A valid asset id is required.
downloadPage.errorReleaseNotFound: Release not found (server release token missing?).

evalDashboard.title: Eval dashboard
evalDashboard.emptyTitle: You haven't run any evals yet.
evalDashboard.emptyHint: Run a scorecard to collect benchmark results.

harnessesPage.title: Harnesses
harnessesPage.description: The agents you evaluate. Run them against any benchmark.
harnessesPage.registerDescription: Register a new harness in this workspace.
harnessesPage.noPermTitle: You don't have permission to register harnesses.
harnessesPage.noPermHint: Only admins can register harnesses. Ask a workspace admin.
harnessesPage.configDescription: The settings this harness actually runs with.
harnessesPage.templateRegisteredNotice: Registered template {template}. Now create a new instance from this version.
harnessesPage.newVersionDescription: Prefilled with {id}'s config. Change the values and register a new version.
harnessesPage.instanceHint: Change only the slot values (image, model) and register a new instance version. The template structure stays the same.
harnessesPage.templateHint: Change the structure to create a new template version. Then create an instance from this version.

harnessList.emptyHint: Try a different search or filter.
harnessList.privateTitle: References personal secrets — only you can see it
harnessList.creator: Created by

importBenchmark.nothingToImport: No benchmarks or recipes to import.
importBenchmark.gatedNotice: This is a gated benchmark. It needs the workspace secret HF_TOKEN to import.
importBenchmark.jsonlRequired: This benchmark needs jsonl file content.
importBenchmark.versionNote: Versions are immutable. Re-importing the same version with different content won't register.
importBenchmark.gradeNoAnswer: No answer-key grading — score it later with a judge or a separate grader.
importBenchmark.promptBody: The agent answers <task></task> from each row.
importBenchmark.browserBodyWithUrl: The agent starts at {url} and does .
importBenchmark.browserBodyNoUrl: The agent does <task></task> in the browser.
importBenchmark.repoBodyNoGit: repo environment but no git field to clone — set it in advanced settings.
importBenchmark.osUseBody: The agent does <task></task> inside the desktop image.
importBenchmark.osUseBodyNoImage: desktop environment but no case image — set it in advanced settings.
importBenchmark.splitsNote: Couldn't fetch config/split info — previewing with the default (train).
importBenchmark.hfConnectHint: If HuggingFace won't connect, paste directly with 'Paste JSONL'.
importBenchmark.dataFileTip: This dataset has no HuggingFace preview server (viewer), so we read the repo's data files directly.
importBenchmark.previewHintGated: This is a gated dataset — register a HuggingFace token in Account → Secrets to import it right away. (You'll also need to accept this dataset's terms on HuggingFace.)
importBenchmark.fieldsFound: Found {count} fields. Click a column header to set its role (task·id·answer).
importBenchmark.cycleRoleTip: Click to set role (task → id → answer → none)
importBenchmark.advancedToggle: Advanced · need it to run differently?
importBenchmark.envKindTip: The world each case runs in. Auto-detection is usually right — change it only when the benchmark actually needs the web (browser), code (repo), or desktop (os-use).
importBenchmark.taskTemplateTip: Combine several fields into the task. '{field}' is replaced with each row's value. Leave empty to use the task field as-is. e.g. to attach where the source doc lives to a question.
importBenchmark.caseImageTip: Set this only when a case must run inside a specific container image (prebuilt repos, desktop, etc.). Pure QA doesn't need it.
importBenchmark.placementTip: Set this only to route these cases to specific execution infra (a runtime). Empty means the default backend.
importBenchmark.createNote: Imports all cases. You can run a subset from the scorecard later.

ingestScorecard.pullDescription: Pull traces from OTel, MLflow, Langfuse, LangSmith, or Phoenix. Don't paste credentials directly — enter a workspace secret name instead.
ingestScorecard.pushTracesHelp: Match caseId to the dataset's cases. Unknown caseIds are skipped. Tool calls and cost are computed automatically from the trace.
ingestScorecard.authSecretHelp: The secret value is used verbatim as the auth header. Don't paste the token — enter a workspace secret name only.
ingestScorecard.pullRunsHelp: Pulls each runId's trace and matches it to the caseId. Tool calls and cost are computed automatically.

inspectHarness.overridesNote: A delta that changes behavior, not structure — merged onto the template at resolve.
inspectHarness.singleProcessBody: Runs an agent CLI (Claude Code · Codex, etc.) directly inside one isolated sandbox. There's no service topology to deploy or slot to pin, so the structure is the version — cost/tokens are aggregated from the harness's own trace.
inspectHarness.runModeBody: LocalDriver runs the harness with the machine's existing login — no separate API key injection.
inspectHarness.isolationBody: The orchestrator's runtime (K8s Job / Nomad alloc) is the isolation boundary — the process runs inside it.

invitePage.title: Workspace invite
invitePage.description: Join a workspace via an invite link.
invitePage.invalidHint: Ask the person who invited you for a new link.
invitePage.invitedAs: You've been invited to this workspace as {role}.

landingPage.heroTitle:
Register a harness, evaluate,

and compare by score.
landingPage.heroSubtitle: Everdict evaluates any agent fairly and safely. People use the web, agents use MCP — same platform.
landingPage.featureAnyAgentBody: Claude Code, Codex, LangGraph… evaluate any agent the same way.
landingPage.featureSafeBody: Isolated per team and run safely within budget.
landingPage.featureCompareBody: See at a glance whether a version got better or worse.

lib.imageHintWorkspace: Workspace registry image — pulls anywhere with the right credentials.
lib.imageHintLocal: Loopback host reference — cannot be pulled outside the machine it was built on.
lib.imageHintUnqualified: Unqualified name — if built locally, other runtimes cannot pull it. Publish it to a workspace registry with everdict image push.

manageApiKeys.title: API keys
manageApiKeys.introRich: Keys agents use to access Everdict (ak_…). You can set permissions when issuing, and a key is shown only once.
manageApiKeys.emptyHintWrite: Issue a new key to grant access to an agent or CI.
manageApiKeys.emptyHintRead: You need admin permission to issue keys.
manageApiKeys.adminRequired: You need admin permission to issue or revoke keys.
manageApiKeys.issuedDesc: Copy the key below now and store it somewhere safe.
manageApiKeys.issuedHint: This key cannot be shown again. Only its prefix is shown afterward.
manageApiKeys.createDesc: Use a label to identify its purpose, and set the permissions for this key.
manageApiKeys.writeDesc: Can run and register, excluding secrets and member management.

manageCiLinks.setupPrFailed: Failed to create the setup PR.
manageCiLinks.panelDescription: Connect a GitHub repo to this harness and CI builds an image and evaluates it automatically on every PR and merge. No keys needed.
manageCiLinks.emptyHintWrite: Click “Connect GitHub repo” to attach a repo.
manageCiLinks.emptyHintRead: An admin sets up repo connections.
manageCiLinks.settingsDescription: A link ties a GitHub repo to a harness. When a link exists, this workspace trusts that repo’s CI, so CI can send evals without any keys. Connect repos and open setup PRs from each harness’s CI integration panel.
manageCiLinks.settingsEmpty: No repos connected yet. Connect one from a harness’s CI integration panel.
manageCiLinks.unlinkAdminRequired: Admin access is required to unlink.
manageCiLinks.reposLoadFailed: Failed to load the repo list.
manageCiLinks.dialogDescription: Connect a repo to the {id} harness and CI builds an image and evaluates it automatically on every PR and merge. The workflow file is created for you via a setup PR.
manageCiLinks.noReposCallout: No repos available to connect. An admin needs to install the GitHub App on the workspace and select repositories for them to show here.
manageCiLinks.installGithubAppLink: Install the GitHub App in Settings → Integrations →
manageCiLinks.connectedMessage: Connected <repoTag>{repo}</repoTag><hostTag>{host}</hostTag> to <harnessTag>{harness}</harnessTag>. Open the setup PR to add the workflow file to the repo. Merging kicks off the CI eval.
manageCiLinks.noSlotsProcess: This harness has no image slots for CI to swap (process). The link only triggers.
manageCiLinks.datasetHelp: Leave it now and it stays a TODO in the setup PR. You can fill it in later.
manageCiLinks.triggerHelp: With the comment mode, leaving /evaluate in the PR conversation runs the eval and replies with the result. Only collaborators and above can trigger it.
manageCiLinks.noRunnersCallout: No shared runners. CI workflows run on self-hosted runners, so you need to register a runner before you can open the setup PR.
manageCiLinks.registerRunnerLink: Register a GitHub Actions runner in Settings → Shared runners →
manageCiLinks.runnersReady: {total} shared runners ({online} online) — builds and evals run on the team runner pool (self:ws).
manageCiLinks.runnersUnavailable: CI workflows run on the workspace’s shared (self-hosted) runners. An admin registers and manages them.
manageCiLinks.runnerHelp: To narrow to a specific runner, set the label (e.g. [self-hosted, everdict-…]) and runtime (e.g. self:ws:…). Leave them blank to grab any runner from the pool.
manageCiLinks.saveAdminRequired: Admin access is required to save a link. Ask a workspace admin.

manageGithubApp.installErrorMissingState: The request info needed to confirm the install is missing. Please start the install again.
manageGithubApp.installErrorInvalidState: The install request has expired or was already used. Please start the install again.
manageGithubApp.installErrorMissingInstallation: GitHub didn’t return installation info. Please start the install again.
manageGithubApp.installErrorInstallFailed: Couldn’t confirm the install. Check the App credentials (for GHE, the registered App ID and private key).
manageGithubApp.title: GitHub App (organization)
manageGithubApp.titleTip: Install the Everdict GitHub App on your organization and pick repositories, and the workspace clones them as a shared team resource (independent of members’ personal logins). Access is limited to the repositories GitHub selected. github.com and GitHub Enterprise both work the same way.
manageGithubApp.description: Connect private repositories per workspace. Only the repositories you pick at install time are accessible.
manageGithubApp.installedNotice: GitHub App install connected. Check the allowed repositories in the install list below.
manageGithubApp.emptyHintCanWrite: Install on your organization via “Install GitHub App” and pick repositories — the install status and allowed repositories show up here.
manageGithubApp.emptyHintReadOnly: Only admins install the GitHub App.
manageGithubApp.noReposHint: No allowed repositories — pick repositories in GitHub’s install settings.
manageGithubApp.gheTip: For GHE you create and register an App on each server yourself. Pick the App private key (PEM) from workspace secrets or save it inline with “New” — only its name is kept in the registration. Once installed, it shows in the install list above just like github.com.

manageImageRegistry.title: Image registries
manageImageRegistry.titleTip: Register your team image registries (GHCR, Harbor, etc.) and harness images are classified as local build or workspace image. Members publish local build images here with <mono>everdict image push</mono> — if you have several, pick one with <mono>--registry '<name>'</mono>, and if there’s only one you can omit it. Pick the token from workspace secrets or save it inline with “New” — only its name is kept here.
manageImageRegistry.description: Register per workspace to use for image provenance classification and publishing.
manageImageRegistry.pushHint: <cmd>everdict image push '<local image>' --registry '<name>'</cmd> — publishes under that registry’s prefix. If you only have one registry, <flag>--registry</flag> can be omitted.
manageImageRegistry.usernamePlaceholder: docker login username — leave blank for token-only registries
manageImageRegistry.pushTokenTip: The token members use to publish with <mono>everdict image push</mono>. Without it, publishing is blocked and only classification works.
manageImageRegistry.missingSecrets: The referenced secrets don’t exist yet: {names} — save them in the Secrets tab and it works right away.

manageInvites.title: Invites
manageInvites.introRich: Create an invite link and send it — the recipient logs in and accepts to join this workspace. The link works only once.
manageInvites.linkOnceHint: This link is shown only once. Copy it now and share it.
manageInvites.adminRequired: You need admin permission to create or revoke invites.

manageMattermost.validationServerToken: Enter the server URL and pick a bot token secret.
manageMattermost.title: Mattermost notifications
manageMattermost.titleTip: Register your Mattermost and it posts run/scorecard completions and regressions to a channel automatically. Pick the bot token from workspace secrets or save it inline with “New” — only its name is kept here.
manageMattermost.description: Register once per workspace to use it for team-wide notifications.
manageMattermost.commandTokenTip: The token issued when you create the <mono>/everdict</mono> slash command in Mattermost. Pick it from workspace secrets or save it inline. Once set, you can run and query from chat, and the inbound URL below is registered on the command.
manageMattermost.inboundUrlTitle: Inbound URL to register in Mattermost
manageMattermost.notConfigured: Mattermost notifications aren’t set up yet.

manageMembers.title: People
manageMembers.description: The people in this workspace. Only admins can change roles or remove members, and the last admin can’t be removed or demoted. Add new members with an invite link.

manageRunners.connectThisDevice: Connect this device as a runner
manageRunners.otherAccountWarning: This device is connected to another account. Reconnecting switches it to a runner on this account.
manageRunners.emptyHintBridge: Connect this device as a runner with one click.
manageRunners.emptyHintDownload: Install the desktop app to connect with one click.

manageSchedules.emptyHint: Try a different search or filter.
manageSchedules.calendarNote: Fire dates of active schedules (approx, UTC)

manageTraceSink.endpointRequired: Enter the platform API base URL.
manageTraceSink.heading: Trace sinks
manageTraceSink.sinkInfoTip: Register the observability platform your team uses, and when scorecard judging finishes, per-case traces and scores are exported there while the scorecard keeps only a summary and shortcut links. You can register multiple sinks, and you pick which one to export to per harness (chosen on the harness detail). If you pulled a trace from that platform and scored it, scores are attached to the original trace instead of duplicating it. For the auth value, pick a workspace secret or save one inline via “New” — only its name is kept here.
manageTraceSink.description: Keep the source of truth for detailed results in your team data lake (MLflow · Langfuse · LangSmith · Phoenix). Pick the sink to export to per harness on the harness detail.
manageTraceSink.name: Name
manageTraceSink.authInfoTip: The value exactly as the platform’s auth header expects it — MLflow/Langfuse use Basic …, Phoenix uses Bearer …, LangSmith the raw API key. Leave it blank for an unauthenticated dev server.
manageTraceSink.webUrlInfoTip: The web UI address used to build shortcut links. Enter it only when it differs from the API address — e.g. LangSmith’s API is api.smith.langchain.com but the UI is smith.langchain.com.

manageWorkspaceRunners.title: Shared runners
manageWorkspaceRunners.description: Runners your team shares (build servers, CI). Once registered, any workspace member can target the runtime as <mono>{target}</mono>. The registration token is shown only once.
manageWorkspaceRunners.emptyHintCanWrite: Register a team build server or CI machine as a shared runner.
manageWorkspaceRunners.emptyHintReadOnly: Admin access is required to register a shared runner.
manageWorkspaceRunners.adminRequired: Admin access is required to register or revoke shared runners.
manageWorkspaceRunners.registeredDesc: Run the command below on the machine you’ll use as the runner to connect it. The token can’t be viewed again.
manageWorkspaceRunners.tokenOnceHint: This token can’t be viewed again. Copy it now and store it safely.
manageWorkspaceRunners.registerDesc: Register a headless runner (build server, CI) for your team to share.
manageWorkspaceRunners.capsHint: The runner probes and re-advertises its real capabilities when it connects — this selection is just an initial label.
manageWorkspaceRunners.githubReadyDesc: Running the script below on your build server brings up both the GitHub runner and the Everdict runner (<mono>{target}</mono>). The script contains a token, so it can’t be viewed again.
manageWorkspaceRunners.registrationExpires: The GitHub registration token is short-lived — run the script soon. Registration expires: {date}
manageWorkspaceRunners.githubRegisterDesc: Set up a GitHub Actions runner and an Everdict shared runner together on one build server. CI builds the image, and the co-located Everdict runner runs the evaluation.
manageWorkspaceRunners.githubAppRequired: Registering a GitHub Actions runner requires the workspace GitHub App. Install it in integration settings, then pick an allowed repo or org right here.
manageWorkspaceRunners.noAllowedRepos: The installed GitHub App can't access any repos. Allow repositories in the App installation.
manageWorkspaceRunners.reposPartialError: Couldn't load repos for some installations: {accounts}
manageWorkspaceRunners.orgShared: An org runner is shared across all repos in that organization.
manageWorkspaceRunners.runnerGroupPlaceholder: Runner group (optional — applies group access policy)
manageWorkspaceRunners.targetMissingHint: Don't see the repo or org you need? Widen the GitHub App's allowed repositories.

workspace.title: Workspace secrets
workspace.desc: Shared keys for runs, grading, and runtime connections — model keys, NOMAD_TOKEN, kubeconfig, etc.
workspace.help: Referenced as “workspace” secrets in harness env and as credential names when registering a runtime. Only the referenced value is used for each purpose.

personal.title: My personal secrets
personal.desc: Keys only you use — other members can’t see them.
personal.help: Reference them with the “personal” scope in harness env, and only you can run or view that harness.

manageWorkspaceSecrets.encryptedNote: Values are encrypted at rest and can’t be viewed again after saving (the list shows names only).
manageWorkspaceSecrets.adminRequired: Admin access is required to make changes.

HF_TOKEN.help: A fine-grained token with read access to the target dataset repo is enough. Gated datasets also need you to accept the terms on your HF account. A personal token takes precedence over the workspace's shared one.

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.help: Used for this workspace's grading calls when a judge uses an Anthropic model.

OPENAI_API_KEY.usedFor: Model judge grading calls (incl. LiteLLM and other compatible gateways)
OPENAI_API_KEY.help: Used when a judge uses an OpenAI (or OpenAI-compatible gateway) model.

manageWorkspaceSecrets.nameInvalid: Must start with an uppercase letter and use only uppercase letters, digits, and underscores.
manageWorkspaceSecrets.saveNote: Once saved, the value can’t be viewed again.

newWorkspacePage.title: New workspace
newWorkspacePage.description: Create a new workspace to keep evaluation data separate. Whoever creates it becomes the admin.

notificationBell.blockedHelp: Your browser blocked notifications for this site. Allow them in the site settings by the address bar, then recheck below.
notificationBell.empty: No notifications yet — they arrive here when work finishes.

onboardingPage.title: Create a workspace to get started
onboardingPage.description: Create a workspace to hold your evaluations. Once it's made, you can start right away.

overviewPage.title: Overview
overviewPage.connectErrorHint: Check that `CONTROL_PLANE_URL` is set and `everdict-api` is running.

palette.placeholder: Go to a page or run an action…

pickSecret.scopeUser: Your personal secret — only you use it; other members can't see it.
pickSecret.scopeWorkspace: Workspace shared secret — members use it together (admins only can register).
pickSecret.nameInvalid: Must start with an uppercase letter and use only uppercase, digits, and underscores.
pickSecret.valueOnce: Once saved, the value can't be viewed again.
pickSecret.nameInvalidServer: The name must start with an uppercase letter and use only uppercase, digits, and underscores.

queueBoard.personalEmpty: No runner connected — <link>connect your machine in Runtimes</link>.

queuePage.title: Queue
queuePage.description: See what's running, what's waiting, and the next scheduled fire per runtime.

registerDataset.tagsHelp: Used as category filters in the list (e.g. coding, browser, qa).
registerDataset.casesHelp: Each case is id · env · task · graders. Any harness is evaluated on the same cases.
registerDataset.immutableNote: Versions are immutable. Re-uploading the same version with different content won't register.
registerDataset.formatOk: ✓ Format OK · {id}@{version} · {cases, plural, one {# case} other {# cases}}
registerDataset.versionExistsNote: — if the content matches it's left as-is, otherwise it won't register.

registerHarness.categoryTip: A label for grouping in the list. kind decides how it runs — category has no effect on execution.
registerHarness.cmdEnvTip: Environment variables for the agent. For secrets like API keys, switch to Secret and reference them — only the name is stored in the spec and the value is injected at run time.
registerHarness.cmdOverrideEnvTip: Override the command harness's env. Reference secrets via Secret.
registerHarness.commandTipPre: A single command line to run the agent.
registerHarness.depExternalNote: external is a store outside Everdict. Everdict doesn't create it — it only passes connection info via env.
registerHarness.depIsolateByTip: How data is isolated so cases don't mix.
registerHarness.depRoleTip: A free-form name for this store's purpose. e.g. main, cache.
registerHarness.depSectionTip: A state store shared across services (DB, cache, etc.). Each case is logically isolated so they don't mix. Leave empty if not needed.
registerHarness.depServiceTip: The service that uses this store. Empty = shared across the whole topology.
registerHarness.descriptionPlaceholder: e.g. add auto-approve prompt flag · bump model to opus-4-8
registerHarness.descriptionTip: A free note on what changed in this version. Shown when viewing this version on the harness detail.
registerHarness.fdServiceTip: The service that receives cases (usually the agent server).
registerHarness.fdSubmitTip: The HTTP request that submits a case. e.g. POST /runs.
registerHarness.fdTraceTip: Path to check completion/trace. Leave empty if none.
registerHarness.frontDoorTip: The entry point where the eval driver submits cases. Sets which service and request cases are sent to.
registerHarness.idTip: The name of this harness (category). e.g. bu, aider, codex.
registerHarness.imagePushHint: publishes it to the workspace registry so it runs anywhere.
registerHarness.imageTip: Container image to run the command. Empty = default sandbox. The instance overrides it via slot.
registerHarness.instanceHint: A template must be registered first. Missing slot pins block registration.
registerHarness.isolateExternal: External/shared store — not deployed by Everdict, connection only
registerHarness.jsonModeNote: Edits made in JSON mode aren't reflected back into the form.
registerHarness.kindCommandDesc: Define a CLI agent like aider/codex with a single command line. Most start here.
registerHarness.kindServiceDesc: Launch multiple containers like an agent server + DB and send cases via front-door. Advanced.
registerHarness.kindTip:
How the harness runs.

command — define with a single CLI line (usually here).

service — a topology launching multiple containers (advanced).
registerHarness.modelTipPre: The default model for the command's
registerHarness.name: Name
registerHarness.pinImageHint: Container image to run the command
registerHarness.pinsCommandTip: A command harness only needs the image and model slots. The template defines the slots.
registerHarness.pinsServiceTip: Set the image per service. Slots are the template's service names.
registerHarness.secretHintSuffix: is injected. Only the name is stored in the spec.
registerHarness.secretHintUserOnly: A personal secret makes this harness visible only to you.
registerHarness.serviceSectionTip: The containers that make up the topology. Each service becomes a slot, and the instance pins an image to it. At least one (usually the agent server) is required.
registerHarness.setupTip: Setup steps to run once before the command. One per line. e.g. pip install aider-chat.
registerHarness.svcEnvTip: Environment variables for this service. For secrets like API keys, switch to Secret and reference them — only the name is stored in the spec.
registerHarness.svcNameTip: The service (container) name. e.g. agent-server, db.
registerHarness.svcNeedsTip: Services that must be up before this one (dependency order). Comma-separated. e.g. db, redis.
registerHarness.svcOverrideEmpty: Override per-service settings like env and replicas. Swap images in Pins above; the service name must exist in the template.
registerHarness.svcOverrideEnvTip: Override this service's env. Reference secrets via Secret.
registerHarness.svcPerRunTip: Key names the runtime injects per case (for isolation). Comma-separated. e.g. thread_id.
registerHarness.svcPortTip: The port this service opens. For an agent server, the front-door sends cases to this port.
registerHarness.svcReadinessIntervalTip: Interval for readiness checks (ms). Empty = runtime default.
registerHarness.svcReadinessTimeoutTip: Max time to wait for this service to become ready (ms). Empty = runtime default.
registerHarness.svcReplicasTip: Number of replicas to launch. Usually 1.
registerHarness.svcSlotTip: The name the instance uses to pin an image. Empty = uses name as the slot.
registerHarness.svcVolumesTip: Volume mounts for the container (docker -v). One per line. e.g. pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data.
registerHarness.tabInstanceHint: An instance is a single harness that pins an image/version to each template slot. Usually one per PR.
registerHarness.tabTemplateHint: A template defines the structure and slots. Pin a version to it to get an instance.
registerHarness.templateIdTip: Which template (category) to build on. Enter an already-registered template id.
registerHarness.templateVersionTip: The template version to use. Enter the version set when the template was registered.
registerHarness.traceEndpointTip: Address to pull traces from. e.g. http://mlflow:5501.
registerHarness.traceSourceTip: Where evaluation pulls the harness-emitted traces (OTel/MLflow) from.
registerHarness.versionExistsNote: If the content is identical it stays; if different, registration is blocked.
registerHarness.workDirTip: Working directory to run the command in. e.g. /tmp.

registerRuntime.kindNomadDescription: Nomad cluster — dispatches the runner agent as an alloc (runsc and other isolation).
registerRuntime.kindK8sDescription: K8s cluster — dispatches as a Job (runtimeClassName isolation).
registerRuntime.idHint: The name this workspace calls the runtime (e.g. prod-k8s).
registerRuntime.versionHint: Immutable. Changing it registers a new version.
registerRuntime.secretHint: Workspace secret "name" (not the value). Register it first under Settings → Secrets.
registerRuntime.contextHint: When authenticating with a local kubeconfig context.
registerRuntime.apiServerHint: When authenticating with a bearer token instead of a context.
registerRuntime.kubeconfigSecretHint: Full kubeconfig secret name for exec-plugin auth (EKS/GKE, etc.).
registerRuntime.topologyToggle: Host service-topology harnesses on this runtime too (topology)
registerRuntime.traceEndpointHint: Source to pull scoring traces from.
registerRuntime.errorNomadRequired: Nomad needs an address (addr) and an image.
registerRuntime.errorTopologyEndpointRequired: Topology support needs a trace endpoint.

reportPage.title: Combined report
reportPage.description: Evaluations from desktop and web at a glance.
reportPage.emptyHint: Run a scorecard and results collect here.

runScorecard.runtimeHelp: Choose where cases run. If you have no runtime or runner registered, register one first.
runScorecard.concurrencyHelp: How many to run at once. Leave empty to use the default.
runScorecard.caseLimitTip: Evaluates only the first N cases instead of all. Leave empty to run everything. The result keeps a partial n/N marker.
runScorecard.caseTagsTip: Evaluates only cases with these tags (comma-separated, matches any).
runScorecard.help: Evaluates every case in the benchmark with this harness and collects the scores. When it finishes, view results on the detail page.

runsPage.title: Activity
runsPage.newRunDescription: Pick a harness and run it once.
runsPage.noPermissionTitle: You don't have permission to start a run.
runsPage.noPermissionHint: Ask a workspace admin for access.

runsTable.emptyHint: Pick a harness and run it to see results here.

runtimesPage.title: Runtimes
runtimesPage.description: Where evaluations run — manage your registered infrastructure and your own machine (self-hosted runner) in one place.
runtimesPage.registeredInfraDescription: docker/nomad/k8s/topology the workspace registered — the control plane connects and places evaluations.
runtimesPage.emptyInfraHint: Use 'Register runtime' to connect workspace infrastructure, or connect your own machine as a runner below.
runtimesPage.myMachineDescription: Your computer connects out to the server and pulls jobs.
runtimesPage.registerDescription: Register execution infrastructure the workspace connects to for placing evaluations. Credentials (tokens, kubeconfig) are referenced by secret name, not by value.

schedulesPage.title: Schedules
schedulesPage.description: {count} · runs automatically on the cadence you set
schedulesPage.emptyHint: Create a schedule and it runs automatically on the cadence you set.
schedulesPage.createDescription: Runs automatically on the cadence you set.
schedulesPage.noPermissionTitle: You don't have permission to create a schedule.
schedulesPage.noPermissionHint: Ask a workspace admin for access.
schedulesPage.editDescription: {name} — change the cadence and run settings

scorecardList.emptyHint: Try a different search or filter.

scorecardsPage.title: Scorecards
scorecardsPage.description: Evaluate a harness against a benchmark in one run and collect the scores.
scorecardsPage.emptyHint: Use "Run scorecard" to evaluate a harness.
scorecardsPage.runDescription: Evaluate a harness against a benchmark and collect the scores.
scorecardsPage.noRunPermTitle: You don't have permission to run.
scorecardsPage.noPermHint: Ask a workspace admin for access.
scorecardsPage.ingestDescription: Upload results run elsewhere to make a scorecard. Ready for compare and leaderboards.
scorecardsPage.noIngestPermTitle: You don't have permission to upload.
scorecardsPage.noCasesFailed: No case results. Check the error above.
scorecardsPage.noCasesRunning: Still running. Case results will appear when it finishes.
scorecardsPage.analyzeCustomDesc: Build the table you want with your own filters, groups, and measures.
scorecardsPage.analyzeEasyDesc: Pick a performance trend, model comparison, or harness comparison and view it instantly.
scorecardsPage.analyzeEmptyHint: Run a scorecard to analyze it here.
scorecardsPage.byHarnessDescription: See the scores and models a harness produced on each benchmark.
scorecardsPage.byHarnessEmptyHint: Evaluate benchmarks with a harness and they'll pile up here.
scorecardsPage.compareDescription: Compare two scorecards to see how the scores changed.
scorecardsPage.needTwoTitle: You need at least 2 completed scorecards to compare.
scorecardsPage.needTwoHint: Evaluate the same benchmark twice to compare.
scorecardsPage.leaderboardDescription: Rank harnesses and models by score on a single benchmark.
scorecardsPage.leaderboardEmptyHint: Register a benchmark and evaluate it with several harnesses to fill the ranking.
scorecardsPage.leaderboardNoMatchHint: Evaluate the selected benchmark with a harness to enter the ranking.
scorecardsPage.trendDescription: See how a benchmark's score changed over time.
scorecardsPage.trendEmptyHint: Evaluate the same benchmark multiple times to build a trend.
scorecardsPage.needTwoPoints: Need at least 2 completed scorecards to draw a trend (currently {count}).

settingsPage.title: Workspace settings
settingsPage.noPermissionTitle: You don't have permission to view settings.
settingsPage.githubHint: Connect private clone/CI via org install and selected repositories.
settingsPage.mattermostHint: Sends completion and regression alerts and the /everdict slash command.
settingsPage.traceSinkHint: Export judged detail to your observability platform (per-harness).
settingsPage.imageRegistryHint: The baseline for image classification and the target for everdict image push.

submitRun.taskPlaceholder: e.g. create ok.txt with the text done
submitRun.runtimeHelp: Choose where it runs. If you have no runtime or runner registered, register one first.
submitRun.gitHelp: Private repos are cloned with automatic auth if the workspace GitHub App is installed on them (Settings › Integrations). Public repos run right away.

ui.envPromptHint: A case answered with text only, no environment
ui.envRepoHint: A case working on files in a code workspace (repo)
ui.envBrowserHint: A case operating a web browser
ui.graderHybridHint: Scored by mixing automated checks with an LLM judge
ui.graderLlmJudgeHint: An LLM scores by reading the trace
ui.graderTestsPassHint: Succeeds when the test command passes
ui.versionFirstHint: First version — bump patch/minor/major from here on.

updateProfile.imageOnly: Only image files can be uploaded.
updateProfile.imageTooLarge: Image is too large. Up to 8MB can be uploaded.
updateProfile.name: Name

viewsPage.title: Views
viewsPage.description: Save analyses you check often and reopen them with fresh data every time.
viewsPage.emptyHint: Build the table you want in the analysis dashboard, then hit 'Save current analysis' and it collects here.
viewsPage.detailDescriptionWorkspace: Workspace-shared view · aggregated live from current data
viewsPage.detailDescriptionPrivate: Private view · aggregated live from current data
viewsPage.emptyScorecardsHint: Run a scorecard and this view fills in.

workspaceSettings.meterUsageHint: Count tokens and cost from this workspace’s runs toward the budget.
workspaceSettings.adminRequired: Admin access is required to change this.
workspaceSettings.imageOnly: Only image files can be uploaded.
workspaceSettings.imageTooLarge: Image is too large. Up to 8 MB is allowed.
workspaceSettings.urlImmutable: The URL is set when the workspace is created and can’t be changed later.
